data.title: In the past 4 hours, the entire network has liquidated $116 million, mainly due to long orders
data.description: Coinglass data shows that in the past four hours, the entire network’s liquidation reached 116 million US dollars, of which long positions liquidated 812.824 billion US dollars and short positions liquidated 35.0273 million US dollars.

data.title: DDC Enterprise increased its holdings by 26 BTC, bringing its total Bitcoin holdings to 1,083.
data.description: DDC Enterprise announced that it has increased its holdings of 26 BTC during the market downturn, with an average purchase price of $108,726. As of now, the company's total Bitcoin holdings have reached 1,083. (Businesswire)

data.title: Peter Schiff Urges Bitcoin Holders to Consider Gold Amid Market Decline
data.description: According to BlockBeats, economist and cryptocurrency critic Peter Schiff recently commented on social media that gold is increasingly capturing Bitcoin's market share. Since reaching a peak in August, Bitcoin has depreciated by 32% when valued against gold. Schiff predicts that the current Bitcoin bear market will be particularly severe and advises holders to sell their Bitcoin and invest in gold instead.

data.title: Ripple acquires GTreasury for $1 billion, expanding into enterprise finance
data.description: Ripple has officially announced its $1 billion acquisition of GTreasury, a financial management system company, marking its foray into the enterprise treasury sector. The deal reportedly marks Ripple's expansion and access to a broad range of enterprise clients, enabling treasury and finance teams to finally put trapped funds to work and process payments in real time.

data.title: Arkham: CI Global has pledged $130 million worth of ETH
data.description: Arkham stated in a post on the X platform that CI Global Asset Management holds $620.61 million worth of ETH in its ETHX ETF, of which $130 million is staked. The ETF currently still holds approximately $490.85 million in unstaked ETH.

data.title: Stablecoins Revolutionize Cross-Border Payments, KPMG Report Finds
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, a report by accounting firm KPMG highlights stablecoins as a significant use case in transforming cross-border payments. Stablecoins have the potential to reduce settlement times from several days to mere seconds and cut transaction costs by up to 99%.

Currently, banks handle approximately $150 trillion annually through slow and costly correspondent banking networks, which tie up substantial funds in nostro and vostro accounts.

The report notes that pioneers like JPMorgan and PayPal are increasingly demanding blockchain-based payment channels, indicating a shift towards more efficient cross-border payment solutions.

data.title: Singularity Guofeng, a Hong Kong-listed company, did not participate in the issuance, promotion, or sale of RWA tokens.
data.description: According to information disclosed by the Hong Kong Stock Exchange, Qidian Guofeng, a Hong Kong-listed company, has issued a supplemental announcement regarding the tokenization of its baijiu (RWA) raw liquor product on a virtual asset operating platform. The announcement states that the company is not involved in the issuance, promotion, or sale of RWA tokens, nor does it provide any financial support, guarantees, or indemnities for the RWA token project. All contractual and regulatory compliance responsibilities rest with Chunlianda, the special purpose vehicle, and its designated service provider. Previously, Qidian Guofeng, a Hong Kong-listed company, disclosed plans to launch a baijiu asset RWA project and issue HK$500 million worth of RWA tokens on the BNB Chain.

data.title: Ethereum(ETH) Drops Below 3,900 USDT with a 1.93% Decrease in 24 Hours
data.description: On Oct 16, 2025, 15:57 PM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, Ethereum has dropped below 3,900 USDT and is now trading at 3,899.580078 USDT, with a narrowed 1.93% decrease in 24 hours.

data.title: Lightspark Launches Grid API for Banks and Developers, Supporting Real-Time Cross-Border Bitcoin Payments
data.description: Lightspark announced the launch of its Grid API for banks and developers, enabling real-time cross-border payments using Bitcoin. The Grid API uses Bitcoin as a hidden settlement layer, enabling instant cross-border payments in 65 countries. The modular platform allows banks, wallets, and developers to transfer funds between fiat currencies, stablecoins, and Bitcoin in real time. (The Block)

data.title: Merlin Chain: BTCFi revenue is growing steadily, and 50% of the revenue will continue to be used for MERL repurchase
data.description: Merlin Chain, the Bitcoin Layer 2 network, announced in a tweet that its BTCFi ecosystem continues to generate stable and sustainable revenue across multiple chains, with revenue coming from staking, liquidity, and yield-generating protocols. According to official plans, over 50% of this revenue will be used to repurchase $MERL tokens. The repurchase mechanism is already in place and will be maintained over the long term. Merlin Chain emphasized that its commitment to BTCFi is not only about ecosystem expansion, but also about returning the tangible value of this growth to the community.

data.title: Coinbase Launches Suite of Business Payment Tools
data.description: According to official news, Coinbase announced that its Coinbase Business has launched a suite of commercial payment tools that enable businesses to send and receive USDC with low fees, instant settlement and global coverage. These tools simplify supplier payments and provide seamless API integration, enabling businesses to scale efficiently.

data.title: Morpho officially launched on Sei Network
data.description: Morpho Labs announced the launch of its lending network on the Sei Network. As a platform managing over $12 billion in deposits, Morpho previously provided enterprise-grade lending services such as crypto-collateralized lending to platforms like Coinbase. This time, the company is integrating its general lending network with the Sei trusted technology ecosystem.

data.title: U.S. September Consumer Demand Slows, Economic Indicators Show
data.description: According to BlockBeats, recent data from credit card transactions and the private sector indicate a slowdown in U.S. consumer demand last month. Economists, after analyzing high-frequency spending data such as credit card borrowing and same-store sales, noted that consumers are beginning to tighten their spending following a robust 4.1% annualized growth in retail activity over the previous three months. Bank of America economist Shruti Mishra highlighted a month-to-month deceleration in spending from June to August, suggesting that the previous growth rate will not be sustained.Data from the analytics platform Second Measure revealed a decline in consumer willingness to purchase non-essential items like furniture, electronics, and appliances last month. Bank of America's credit card data also reflected a cooling in demand. Barclays economists indicated that, based on a model incorporating disposable income, stock market wealth, inflation, consumer confidence, and credit card spending, the momentum of retail sales in September "may have weakened."
